questions.prompt: Tell us about yourself—anything and everything you’d like us to know so we can create the best roommate match for you.
You're trying to finish an important assignment when your roommate starts watching videos out loud. What would that be like for you?

options.label: I'd be distracted until the room was quiet.
I'd be fine if they switched to headphones.
That kind of background noise usually doesn't bother me.
I'd rather head somewhere else to focus.
Something else (tell us what it would be like).

questions.prompt: It's the night before an early class, and your roommate is still moving around and getting ready for bed. How does that sit with you?

options.label: I'd have a hard time settling down unless it was very quiet.
A little occasional noise would be okay.
Normal room noise wouldn't keep me up.
It really depends on the day.
Something else (tell us what it would be like).

questions.prompt: You and your roommate both have a free evening at home. What kind of roommate dynamic feels right to you?

options.label: I'd love for us to hang out regularly, like friends.
Friendly and social sometimes is the sweet spot for me.
I'm most comfortable when we're respectful but do our own thing.
I could be comfortable with any of these.
Something else (tell us what it would be like).

questions.prompt: After a long day of classes, you get back to your shared room. How much space to yourself helps you recharge?

options.label: Being around someone would probably energize me.
A mix of shared time and solo time keeps me balanced.
I need regular, uninterrupted time alone to reset.
I feel best when home is mostly private time.
Something else (tell us what it would be like).

questions.prompt: Your roommate's weekday schedule is pretty different from yours. Which routine would be easiest for you to live alongside?

options.label: Early nights and early mornings work best for me.
A mostly daytime schedule feels natural to me.
I'm more at ease with late nights and late mornings.
I'm okay when schedules change a lot.
Something else (tell us what it would be like).

questions.prompt: Your roommate needs to be up late during a week when you have early mornings. What kind of solution would leave you feeling okay?

options.label: Clear quiet hours would take the stress off me.
I'd feel supported if we both made small adjustments.
I'd be most comfortable using separate spaces when we can.
I'm okay figuring it out as each situation comes up.
Something else (tell us what it would be like).

questions.prompt: Your roommate has traditions or daily routines that are new to you. What's your honest first reaction?

options.label: I'd be curious and interested in learning more.
I'd respect it, even if we mostly kept our routines separate.
I'd be more at ease with clear ground rules for shared space.
I'd need a little time to understand what works for both of us.
Something else (tell us what it would be like).

questions.prompt: A difference in routines turns into a misunderstanding between you and your roommate. What would help you feel comfortable moving forward?

options.label: Talking it through openly would reassure me.
I'd rather cool off first, then talk.
Going back to a shared agreement would make me feel more secure.
I'd feel supported with a neutral person helping us talk.
Something else (tell us what it would be like).

questions.prompt: The sink is full, the trash needs to go out, and both of you are busy. What kind of chore setup would make home feel manageable?

options.label: A regular schedule would help me relax.
I'd feel better knowing exactly what each person handles.
I'm comfortable taking care of things as they come up.
I'd rather decide together and adjust when needed.
Something else (tell us what it would be like).

questions.prompt: Your roommate asks to use some detergent, snacks, and other supplies you bought. What kind of sharing arrangement feels comfortable?

options.label: I feel best keeping most of my things separate.
I'm happy to share the things we've agreed on.
Sharing most everyday supplies feels easy to me.
I'd rather decide based on the item.
Something else (tell us what it would be like).

questions.prompt: You've cleaned the shared kitchen a few times, and your roommate keeps leaving it messy. How would you want to deal with that?

options.label: I'd feel better bringing it up directly soon.
I'd want a little time to cool off before talking.
A message first would feel less uncomfortable for me.
I'd feel better setting aside time to work out a solution.
Something else (tell us what it would be like).

questions.prompt: A small roommate issue comes up right before you both leave for class. When would you feel best talking about it?

options.label: I'd rather clear the air before we leave.
Later that day would give me time to think.
I'd feel more ready if we picked a time to talk.
It depends on how serious the issue is.
Something else (tell us what it would be like).
